paper_title: Adapter-Enhanced Semantic Prompting for Continual Learning

method_section.problem_statement: Continual Learning (CL) suffers from catastrophic forgetting, where old knowledge is overwritten when learning new tasks, leading to a significant decline in performance for previous tasks.

existing_approaches.mechanism: Retain a few representative samples from old tasks to be replayed with new task data.
Add new branches to the network for each new task.
limitations: Results in slower inference speed and lower efficiency.

existing_approaches.mechanism: Learn visual prompts to adapt a pre-trained Visual Transformer (ViT) model to CL tasks.
limitations: May struggle with generalization, especially with limited and non-diverse training data.

proposed_solution.name: Adapter-Enhanced Semantic Prompting (AESP) framework
proposed_solution.core_idea: Introduce semantic information to enhance the generalization of a pre-trained ViT model for more adaptive visual feature learning.

key_components.function: To enrich semantic information by using class semantic information extracted from large language models.
key_components.purpose: To create a more generalized feature space, enabling the model to focus on shared semantic features rather than specific visual ones, thus improving generalization.

key_components.function: Small, learnable modules integrated into each layer of the ViT.
key_components.purpose: To help the model accommodate semantic information and facilitate effective interaction between visual and semantic features, addressing their differing representations and feature spaces.

title: Adapter-Enhanced Semantic Prompting for Continual Learning
